seo.title: Vercel AI SDK Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Vercel AI SDK agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using Vercel AI SDK face heightened compliance risks due to the SDK's streaming responses and multi-provider tool calling, which can expose unvalidated financial advice or transaction data. Without guardrails, partial hallucinations in streams or inconsistent structured outputs can lead to regulatory violations under SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC rules. Vex provides middleware to enforce validation, encryption checks, and disclaimers before content reaches users.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over AI-generated data used in disclosures.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Intercepts generateText calls to validate factual accuracy and audit AI outputs for SOX-compliant logging.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption of cardholder data at rest and in transit, prohibiting exposure in AI prompts or responses.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Scans streamText payloads for PII/card data and enforces encryption policies before transmission.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires explainability for automated decisions affecting users and right to explanation for AI outputs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Adds provenance tracking and explanation metadata to structured tool call outputs.

complianceRequirements.description: Financial advice must include clear disclaimers and cannot recommend unsuitable investments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Middleware injects mandatory disclaimers and validates advice against customer suitability profiles.

industryRisks.name: Streaming Hallucinations
industryRisks.description: Partial AI responses during financial advice streaming contain incorrect investment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: AI suggests 'buy XYZ stock' mid-stream before correcting, user acts on partial info causing losses.

industryRisks.name: Unvalidated Tool Calls
industryRisks.description: Transaction approval tools execute without result validation, exposing fraud vectors.
industryRisks.example: AI calls payment API with tampered amount due to unverified provider response.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Structured Output
industryRisks.description: Different LLM providers return varying transaction risk scores, leading to approval inconsistencies.
industryRisks.example: OpenAI flags high risk, Anthropic approves same AML check, causing compliance violations.

industryRisks.name: Edge Timeout Failures
industryRisks.description: Complex credit scoring agents timeout on Vercel Edge, leaving decisions uncompleted.
industryRisks.example: Credit approval hangs during peak traffic, violating fair lending explainability requirements.

implementation.title: Add Vex Middleware to streamText for Hallucination Guardrails
implementation.description: Wrap Vercel AI SDK's streamText with Vex to validate streaming financial advice and inject disclaimers.

implementation.title: Secure Tool Calls with PCI Validation
implementation.description: Protect transaction APIs by validating tool call results before execution in fintech workflows.

implementation.title: Normalize Structured Outputs Across Providers
implementation.description: Ensure consistent credit decision schemas regardless of underlying LLM provider.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Vercel AI SDK in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: NeoBank, a digital-first neobank, deployed Vercel AI SDK to power their intelligent chat assistant for loan applications and investment advice. The streaming UI provided real-time credit scoring and personalized portfolio recommendations using generateText and streamText across multiple LLM providers. However, during beta testing, they encountered critical issues: streaming responses occasionally hallucinated stock recommendations mid-conversation, tool calls for transaction approvals lacked validation exposing PCI data, and edge functions timed out during complex AML risk assessments, leaving customers with incomplete decisions.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex middleware, NeoBank achieved 100% compliance coverage. Vex automatically injected SEC-mandated disclaimers, validated all tool call parameters against PCI DSS encryption rules, and normalized structured outputs across providers for consistent credit decisions. Edge timeouts dropped 92% through intelligent task queuing, preventing regulatory violations and earning SOC 2 Type II certification within 90 days.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Vercel AI SDK deployment in 5 minutes to eliminate compliance gaps.
